rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they refer to visible ports or features.
Use visible identifiers from the AADL model to define assumptions and guarantees.
generic_rules: Use explicit type declarations in AGREE equations to match AADL data types.
Avoid encoding AADL properties as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed as ports or constants.
Write deterministic dependency guarantees that relate visible inputs to visible outputs.
Generate range assumptions using named constants or visible feature names when bounds are provided.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};`.
Assumptions and guarantees must be syntactically valid AGREE expressions.
Constants and equations in AGREE should use proper type annotations.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL property names like Compute_Execution_Time directly in AGREE expressions.
Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type contracts.
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers unless the port is explicitly typed as bool.
irrelevant_topics: AADL model fusion and owner/scope preservation rules
